Off Calendar Aug-25-2014 Continued To Dec-01-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Drexler for and with Jack Blanks (appeared by telephone).

Mr. Drexler stated that they are very close to being able to file the final petition and that they are waiting for clearance from the taxing facility. Status on filing of a petition for final distribution was continued to December 1, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Co-administrator and the Attorney to appear on August 25, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the petition for final distribution has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-15-2010 Continued To Jan-26-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Drexler for Jack Blanks (Petitioner).

Mr. Drexler stated that he was appearing with the approval of the Attorney representing Mr. Garrison, that the matter settled, that he filed the stipulation and that he has an order seeking appointment of both Petitioners as Co-Aministrators with the understanding that the will is to be proved. In addition, he stated that he has a waiver of bond signed by both Beneficiaries. The Court stated that the proper way to proceed is to file an ex parte petition to appoint Co-Special Administrators. The matter was continued to January 26, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Dec-15-2010 Continued To Jan-26-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-17-2010 Continued To Dec-15-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Aaron Kamins for Stephen Garrison. Chalres Drexler for and with Jack Blanks (Petitioner) and for Project Hope.

Discussions were held regarding the manner in which to proceed in order to obtain access to the Decedent's apartment for the purpose of proving the will. The matter was continued to December 15, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to contemplate if a new Petition for Appointment of Special Administrator is to be filed, whether the Parties can be appointed as Co-Administrators, or if the Parties can reach a stipulation with regard to Stephen Garrison being appointed as Administrator with will annexed with a bond increase to $105,000.00 provided entry into the apartment is performed in Mr. Blanks presence.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Aguirre.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Nov-17-2010 Continued To Dec-15-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Nov-09-2010 Continued To Nov-17-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-12-2010 Continued To Oct-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-22-2010 Continued To Oct-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Aaron kamins for and with Stephen Garrison (Petitioner).

The Court stated that a declaration from Mr. Kamins with regard to the attestation clause would satisfy part B of the code section and asked if there was someone that could recognize the Decedent's signature to satisfy part A of the code section. Mr. Kamins stated that Mr. Garrison has had no contact with anyone, that all of the Executors and the Witnesses were deceased, and requested Letters of Special Administration for authority to gain access to the apartment. Mr. Garrison explained the difficulty of gaining access to the Decedent's personal property, stated that he was getting hit with rents and that he had no access to the credit card information. The matter was continued to October 12, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Kamins will file an Ex Parte Petition for Special Administrator.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
